title: Bees on the roof
summary: On a hotel roof in Toronto, a beekeeper shows why city bees can make sweeter honey than country bees.
body:
High above the streets of Toronto, there is a garden on a roof. It is on top of a tall hotel. On the roof, there are bee boxes. Inside the boxes, thousands of bees live and work. A beekeeper named Daniel takes care of them.

Daniel wears a white suit and a hat with a net. He opens a box. The bees fly around him, but they are calm. 'People think the city is bad for bees,' he said. 'But it is not. The city is good for them.'

This is the surprise. In the country, farms grow only one plant. The bees eat from one flower. But the city has many small gardens, parks, and balconies. Many different flowers grow there. The bees fly from flower to flower. They taste many things. So the honey from the roof is rich and sweet.

The bees also help the city. They carry pollen from plant to plant. The plants make more fruit and seeds. The garden on the roof grows well. People in the hotel eat the honey at breakfast. They smile. They do not know the busy little workers are right above their heads.

grammarNotes.title: Present simple for facts
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'present simple' cho sự thật và thói quen: 'thousands of bees live and work', 'The bees fly from flower to flower.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple for facts and habits: 'thousands of bees live and work' and 'The bees fly from flower to flower.'

grammarNotes.title: There is / There are
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'there is' cho số ít và 'there are' cho số nhiều: 'there is a garden on a roof' và 'there are bee boxes'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'there is' for one thing and 'there are' for many: 'there is a garden on a roof' and 'there are bee boxes'.

translationVi:
Cao trên những con phố của Toronto, có một khu vườn trên mái nhà. Nó nằm trên đỉnh một khách sạn cao. Trên mái nhà có những thùng ong. Bên trong các thùng, hàng nghìn con ong sống và làm việc. Một người nuôi ong tên Daniel chăm sóc chúng.

Daniel mặc một bộ đồ trắng và đội mũ có lưới. Anh ấy mở một thùng ong. Đàn ong bay quanh anh, nhưng chúng rất yên. 'Người ta nghĩ thành phố không tốt cho ong,' anh ấy nói. 'Nhưng không phải vậy. Thành phố tốt cho chúng.'

Đây là điều bất ngờ. Ở vùng quê, các nông trại chỉ trồng một loại cây. Ong chỉ ăn từ một loài hoa. Nhưng thành phố có nhiều khu vườn nhỏ, công viên và ban công. Nhiều loài hoa khác nhau mọc ở đó. Ong bay từ hoa này sang hoa khác. Chúng nếm nhiều thứ. Vì vậy mật ong từ mái nhà đậm đà và ngọt ngào.

Đàn ong cũng giúp ích cho thành phố. Chúng mang phấn hoa từ cây này sang cây khác. Cây cối tạo ra nhiều trái và hạt hơn. Khu vườn trên mái nhà phát triển tốt. Khách trong khách sạn ăn mật ong vào bữa sáng. Họ mỉm cười. Họ không biết rằng những người thợ nhỏ bận rộn đang ở ngay trên đầu họ.

questions.explanation: 'there is a garden on a roof' on top of a hotel in Toronto.

questions.prompt: What lives in the bee boxes?
questions.explanation: 'thousands of bees live and work' inside the boxes.

questions.prompt: Who takes care of the bees?
questions.explanation: 'A beekeeper named Daniel takes care of them.'

options: A white suit and a hat with a net
questions.explanation: 'Daniel wears a white suit and a hat with a net.'

questions.prompt: What do many people wrongly think?
options: The city is good for bees
The city is bad for bees
questions.explanation: 'People think the city is bad for bees,' but Daniel says it is good.

questions.prompt: Why do country bees eat from only one flower?
questions.explanation: 'In the country, farms grow only one plant.'

questions.prompt: Why is the city good for bees?
questions.explanation: 'Many different flowers grow there' in city gardens and parks.

questions.prompt: What is the honey from the roof like?
questions.explanation: 'the honey from the roof is rich and sweet'.
questions.hint: Look at the end of the third paragraph.

options: Make plants grow more fruit and seeds
questions.explanation: Bees carry pollen so 'the plants make more fruit and seeds'.

questions.prompt: Do the hotel guests know the bees are above them?
questions.explanation: 'They do not know the busy little workers are right above their heads.'

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: City bees can make sweeter honey because the city has many flowers
The country is always better for bees
questions.explanation: The surprise is that the varied city is good for bees and their honey.

questions.prompt: Who are the 'busy little workers' in the last line?
questions.explanation: The 'busy little workers' are the bees on the roof.
questions.hint: Think about who works above their heads.

blanks.explanation: 'They carry pollen from plant to plant.'
'People in the hotel eat the honey at breakfast.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: Most people think the country is better for bees than the city, but the passage shows the opposite. Why is it easy to believe an 'obvious' idea that turns out to be wrong? What helped Daniel see the truth?
